chunk_summary: This section focuses on the ten-step care model for managing severe acute malnutrition (SAM), emphasizing stabilization and rehabilitation phases based on WHO and national guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the ten-step care model for managing SAM in children.
Identify key interventions during the stabilization and rehabilitation phases.
Learn evidence-based guidelines for preventing and managing wasting and nutritional oedema.
Apply operational protocols for facility-based management of SAM.
key_facts: The ten-step care model includes critical interventions such as treating hypoglycemia, hypothermia, and dehydration.
Stabilization phase focuses on life-threatening conditions, while rehabilitation emphasizes nutritional recovery and growth.
WHO guidelines recommend small, frequent feeds during the stabilization phase to prevent refeeding syndrome.
Nutritional oedema management requires careful monitoring and therapeutic feeding protocols.
Facility-based management of SAM is outlined in the 2011 Indian Ministry of Health guidelines.
SAM affects millions of children globally, with a high risk of mortality if untreated.
reference_quote: The ten-step care model for SAM management includes treating hypoglycemia, hypothermia, dehydration, and infections, followed by nutritional rehabilitation and catch-up growth. (Source: WHO Pocket Book of Hospital Care for Children, https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK154454/)
